nodes.description: Short fingers are a characteristic hand phenotype in achondroplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001156 | Brachydactyly | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: C-type natriuretic peptide (CNP) signaling through NPR-B (NPR2) physiologically antagonizes FGFR3-MAPK activity in growth plate chondrocytes. CNP inhibits the MAPK pathway downstream of FGFR3, correcting decreased extracellular matrix synthesis but not affecting the STAT1-mediated proliferation defect. This counter-regulatory pathway provides the mechanistic rationale for CNP analog therapy (vosoritide).

evidence.reference_title: Overexpression of CNP in chondrocytes rescues achondroplasia through a MAPK-dependent pathway.
evidence.snippet: CNP prevented the shortening of achondroplastic bones by correcting the decreased extracellular matrix synthesis in the growth plate through inhibition of the MAPK pathway of FGF signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Overexpression of CNP in chondrocytes rescues achondroplasia through a MAPK-dependent pathway.
evidence.snippet: CNP had no effect on the STAT-1 pathway of FGF signaling that mediates the decreased proliferation and the delayed differentiation of achondroplastic chondrocytes.

nodes.description: Central sleep apnea occurs in some affected children and is part of the sleep-disordered breathing spectrum in achondroplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of achondroplasia: A retrospective review of longitudinal clinical data.
evidence.snippet: Central sleep apnea and obstructive sleep apnea were present in children, while the diagnosis of obstructive sleep apnea was shown to recur in adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Sleep-disordered breathing in children with achondroplasia assessed by polysomnography: a retrospective chart review.
evidence.snippet: Sleep-disordered breathing subtypes included obstructive sleep apnoea in 81% (55/68), central sleep apnoea in 3% (2/68), mixed sleep apnoea in 7% (5/68) and primary snoring in 9% (6/68).

nodes.description: Hearing loss is common and is usually conductive or mixed rather than purely sensorineural.

evidence.reference_title: Otologic Manifestations in Patients with Achondroplasia: A Multicenter Study.
evidence.snippet: Among 53 patients who underwent audiometry, 26 showed conductive hearing loss, 2 had mixed-type hearing loss, and 4 had sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Hearing loss in Norwegian adults with achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: The majority (15/24) had conductive hearing loss, or a combination of conductive and sensorineural hearing loss (8/24).
HP:0000365 | Hearing impairment | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: FGFR3-MAPK activation accelerates closure of cranial base synchondroses and fusion of ossification centers, leading to premature cranial base fusion and narrowing of the foramen magnum. This mechanism underlies the foramen magnum stenosis that poses a risk of cervicomedullary compression and sudden death in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: FGFR3 promotes synchondrosis closure and fusion of ossification centers through the MAPK pathway.
evidence.snippet: We observed premature synchondrosis closure in the spine and cranial base in human cases of homozygous achondroplasia and thanatophoric dysplasia as well as in mouse models of achondroplasia.

evidence.reference_title: FGFR3 promotes synchondrosis closure and fusion of ossification centers through the MAPK pathway.
evidence.snippet: Chondrocyte-specific activation of Fgfr3 in mice induced premature synchondrosis closure and enhanced osteoblast differentiation around synchondroses.

evidence.reference_title: Constitutive activation of MEK1 in chondrocytes causes Stat1-independent achondroplasia-like dwarfism and rescues the Fgfr3-deficient mouse phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Immunohistochemical analysis of the cranial base in transgenic embryos showed reduced staining for collagen type X and persistent expression of Sox9 in chondrocytes.

nodes.description: Children with achondroplasia show delayed acquisition of gross motor and ambulatory skills.

evidence.reference_title: Development in children with achondroplasia: a prospective clinical cohort study.
evidence.snippet: children with achondroplasia are delayed in development of gross motor and ambulatory skills.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: There was motor developmental delay in 18 patients and speech delay in 16 patients.

nodes.description: Speech and language delay is reported in pediatric achondroplasia cohorts.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: There was motor developmental delay in 18 patients and speech delay in 16 patients.

evidence.reference_title: Development in children with achondroplasia: a prospective clinical cohort study.
evidence.snippet: While delays were seen in development of later communication items, there were fewer delays seen across development of early communication, fine motor, and feeding skills.

nodes.description: Depressed nasal bridge is part of the characteristic midface hypoplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005280 | Depressed nasal bridge | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Disproportionate short-limb short stature is a defining skeletal manifestation of achondroplasia.

evidence.snippet: Achondroplasia is a genetic disorder that results in disproportionate short stature.

nodes.description: Elbow contractures are recognized childhood orthopedic manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of achondroplasia: A retrospective review of longitudinal clinical data.
evidence.snippet: Cervical and lumbar stenosis were diagnosed in children and adults while, genu varum, elbow contractures, and radial head dislocations were identified during childhood.

evidence.reference_title: FGFR3 / achondroplasia (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: FGFR3 | HGNC:3690 | achondroplasia | MONDO:0007037 | AD | Definitive

evidence.reference_title: Achondroplasia is defined by recurrent G380R mutations of FGFR3.
evidence.snippet: The homogeneity of mutations in achondroplasia is unprecedented for an autosomal dominant disorder and may explain the relative lack of heterogeneity in the achondroplasia phenotype.
FGFR3 | fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 | hgnc:3690 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (gain of function) in

nodes.description: The FGFR3 G380R mutation causes constitutive activation of the receptor, leading to enhanced downstream signaling through STAT1, MAPK/ERK, and p38 MAPK pathways. This results in premature cell cycle arrest of growth plate chondrocytes, reduced chondrocyte proliferation and hypertrophy, and impaired endochondral bone formation. The receptor normally serves as a negative regulator of bone growth; its overactivation leads to the shortened long bones characteristic of achondroplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the transmembrane domain of FGFR3 cause the most common genetic form of dwarfism, achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: DNA studies revealed point mutations in the FGFR3 gene in ACH heterozygotes and homozygotes. The mutation on 15 of the 16 ACH-affected chromosomes was the same, a G-->A transition, at nucleotide 1138 of the cDNA.

evidence.reference_title: Achondroplasia is defined by recurrent G380R mutations of FGFR3.
evidence.snippet: All but one, an atypical case, were found to have a glycine-to-arginine substitution at codon 380. Of these, 150 had a G-to-A transition at nt 1138, and 3 had a G-to-C transversion at this same position.
FGFR3 | fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 | hgnc:3690 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) (gain of function) in

pdb_structures.description: FGFR3 kinase domain crystal structure showing the constitutively active conformation caused by achondroplasia-associated pathogenic mutations

nodes.description: Infantile hypotonia is frequently observed and contributes to delayed motor milestones.

evidence.snippet: HP:0008947 | Floppy infant | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Foramen magnum stenosis is a major infant complication that can cause cervical cord compression.

evidence.reference_title: Achondroplasia Foramen Magnum Score: screening infants for stenosis.
evidence.snippet: Of 36 infants (M:F, 18:18), 2 (5.6%) did not have FMS (AFMS0); 13 (36.1%) had FMS with preservation of the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) spaces (AFMS1); 3 (8.3%) had FMS with loss of the CSF space but no spinal cord distortion (AFMS2); 13 (36.1%) had FMS with flattening of the cervical cord without signal change (AFMS3); and 5 (13.9%) had FMS resulting in cervical cord signal change (AFMS4).

evidence.reference_title: Clinical outcomes and medical management of achondroplasia in Japanese children: A retrospective medical record review of clinical data.
evidence.snippet: All patients were monitored by magnetic resonance imaging; 73.0% had foramen magnum stenosis, while 54.1% had Achondroplasia Foramen Magnum Score 3 or 4.

nodes.description: Frontal bossing is a characteristic craniofacial feature of achondroplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002007 | Frontal bossing | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Genu varum is a common lower-limb deformity and is typically recognized during childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent clinical findings were short stature, high forehead, trident hands, genu varum and macrocephaly.

nodes.description: Hip joint hypermobility is a frequent finding in achondroplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0045087 | Hip joint hypermobility | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hydrocephalus is an occasional but clinically important neurosurgical complication in childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Predictors of cervical myelopathy and hydrocephalus in young children with achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: Cervical myelopathy and hydrocephalus occasionally occur in young children with achondroplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: One patient developed hydrocephalus at 10 years old.
HP:0000238 | Hydrocephalus | Very rare (<4-1%)

nodes.description: In the growth plate, FGFR3 overactivation disrupts the normal columnar organization of chondrocytes and reduces the height of the proliferative and hypertrophic zones. This results in shortened and disorganized growth plates with reduced longitudinal bone growth, particularly affecting the long bones of the limbs (rhizomelic pattern) and the skull base (leading to foramen magnum stenosis).

evidence.reference_title: Overexpression of FGFR3, Stat1, Stat5 and p21Cip1 correlates with phenotypic severity and defective chondrocyte differentiation in FGFR3-related chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Immunohistochemical analysis of fetal growth plates showed a phenotype-related reduction of the collagen type X-positive hypertrophic zone.

nodes.description: Knee joint hypermobility is a frequent finding contributing to genu varum and gait disturbance.

evidence.snippet: HP:0045086 | Knee joint hypermobility | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Limb undergrowth is the core skeletal manifestation of achondroplasia, reflecting impaired endochondral ossification of the appendicular skeleton.

evidence.snippet: HP:0009826 | Limb undergrowth | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Limited elbow extension is a common upper-limb joint restriction in achondroplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001377 | Limited elbow extension | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Exaggerated lumbar lordosis is a characteristic spinal deformity in achondroplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002938 | Lumbar hyperlordosis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: The MAPK/ERK cascade is a principal effector of FGFR3-driven growth inhibition. Constitutive activation of MEK1 in chondrocytes causes achondroplasia-like dwarfism with incomplete hypertrophy and delayed endochondral ossification, while chondrocyte proliferation remains unaffected. The MAPK pathway inhibits hypertrophic differentiation, and STAT1 mediates inhibition of chondrocyte proliferation as a parallel pathway. Upregulation of FGFR3, Stat1, Stat5, and p21Cip1 in the prehypertrophic-hypertrophic zone correlates with disease severity and impaired chondrocyte differentiation.

evidence.reference_title: Constitutive activation of MEK1 in chondrocytes causes Stat1-independent achondroplasia-like dwarfism and rescues the Fgfr3-deficient mouse phenotype.
evidence.snippet: These observations indicate that the MAPK pathway inhibits hypertrophic differentiation of chondrocytes and negatively regulates bone growth without inhibiting chondrocyte proliferation.

evidence.reference_title: Constitutive activation of MEK1 in chondrocytes causes Stat1-independent achondroplasia-like dwarfism and rescues the Fgfr3-deficient mouse phenotype.
evidence.snippet: These observations suggest a model in which Fgfr3 signaling inhibits bone growth by inhibiting chondrocyte differentiation through the MAPK pathway and by inhibiting chondrocyte proliferation through Stat1.

evidence.reference_title: Overexpression of FGFR3, Stat1, Stat5 and p21Cip1 correlates with phenotypic severity and defective chondrocyte differentiation in FGFR3-related chondrodysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Our results indicate that FGFR3 mutations in the prenatal period upregulate FGFR3 and Stat-p21Cip1 expression, thus inducing premature exit of proliferative cells from the cell cycle and their differentiation into prehypertrophic chondrocytes.

nodes.description: Macrocephaly is part of the characteristic cranial phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent clinical findings were short stature, high forehead, trident hands, genu varum and macrocephaly.
HP:0000256 | Macrocephaly | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Midface retrusion contributes to the characteristic craniofacial appearance.

evidence.reference_title: Craniofacial growth and function in achondroplasia: a multimodal 3D study on 15 patients.
evidence.snippet: Craniofacial phenotype was characterized by maxillo-zygomatic retrusion, deep nasal root, and prominent forehead.

nodes.description: Obstructive sleep apnea is a major respiratory complication in children and can recur later in life.

evidence.reference_title: Sleep-disordered breathing in children with achondroplasia assessed by polysomnography: a retrospective chart review.
evidence.snippet: Sleep-disordered breathing subtypes included obstructive sleep apnoea in 81% (55/68), central sleep apnoea in 3% (2/68), mixed sleep apnoea in 7% (5/68) and primary snoring in 9% (6/68).

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of achondroplasia: A retrospective review of longitudinal clinical data.
evidence.snippet: Central sleep apnea and obstructive sleep apnea were present in children, while the diagnosis of obstructive sleep apnea was shown to recur in adulthood.
HP:0002870 | Obstructive sleep apnea | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Otitis media with effusion is a common otologic complication and often leads to repeated ventilation-tube placement.

evidence.reference_title: Otologic Manifestations in Patients with Achondroplasia: A Multicenter Study.
evidence.snippet: Fifty-one patients (72.9%) had middle ear effusion at least once.

nodes.description: Prominent forehead is a characteristic craniofacial feature.

evidence.reference_title: Craniofacial growth and function in achondroplasia: a multimodal 3D study on 15 patients.
evidence.snippet: Craniofacial phenotype was characterized by maxillo-zygomatic retrusion, deep nasal root, and prominent forehead.

nodes.description: Radial head dislocation is an orthopedic manifestation recognized during childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of achondroplasia: A retrospective review of longitudinal clinical data.
evidence.snippet: Cervical and lumbar stenosis were diagnosed in children and adults while, genu varum, elbow contractures, and radial head dislocations were identified during childhood.

nodes.description: Restrictive lung disease can occur due to thoracic hypoplasia and reduced chest wall compliance.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002091 | Restrictive ventilatory defect | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Shortening is most pronounced in the proximal long bones. Orphanet codes the strict HPO term HP:0008905 (Rhizomelia) as Occasional; the broader rhizomelic shortening pattern is universally present but captured under Disproportionate short stature and Limb undergrowth.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent radiographic findings were rhizomelic shortening of the long bones and narrowing of the interpediculate distance of the caudal spine.
HP:0008905 | Rhizomelia | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Scoliosis is a common spinal deformity in achondroplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence of Scoliosis and Thoracolumbar Kyphosis in Patients With Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: Scoliosis was observed in 60%.

nodes.description: Cervical and lumbar spinal canal stenosis occur across the lifespan and symptomatic lumbar disease is especially important in adults.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of achondroplasia: A retrospective review of longitudinal clinical data.
evidence.snippet: Cervical and lumbar stenosis were diagnosed in children and adults while, genu varum, elbow contractures, and radial head dislocations were identified during childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Lumbar spinal stenosis and disc alterations affect the upper lumbar spine in adults with achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: Unlike the general population, spinal stenosis and disc degeneration involve the upper part of the lumbar spine in adults with achondroplasia, associated with thoraco-lumbar kyphosis and loss of lumbar lordosis.
HP:0003416 | Spinal canal stenosis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Thoracolumbar kyphosis is common in young children and often improves over time.

evidence.reference_title: Walking status and spinopelvic parameters in young children with achondroplasia: 10-year follow-up.
evidence.snippet: Thoracolumbar kyphosis (TLK) is common in children with achondroplasia and resolves in 90% by 10 years of age.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence of Scoliosis and Thoracolumbar Kyphosis in Patients With Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: Thoracolumbar kyphosis was observed in 79%, with 52% exhibiting moderate to severe curvature.
HP:0005619 | Thoracolumbar kyphosis | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Trident hand is a characteristic hand configuration in achondroplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of 39 patients with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent clinical findings were short stature, high forehead, trident hands, genu varum and macrocephaly.
HP:0004060 | Trident hand | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: C-type natriuretic peptide (CNP) analog that counteracts FGFR3 overactivation by stimulating the NPR-B receptor, promoting endochondral bone growth. FDA approved in 2021 for children aged 5 years and older with open growth plates. Phase 3 extension data demonstrated sustained growth-promoting effects for up to 6 years, with a mean additional height gain of 5.75 cm over 3 years compared to untreated controls.

evidence.reference_title: C-Type Natriuretic Peptide Analogue Therapy in Children with Achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: Treatment resulted in a sustained increase in the annualized growth velocity for up to 42 months.

evidence.reference_title: Sustained growth-promoting effects of vosoritide in children with achondroplasia from an ongoing phase 3 extension study.
evidence.snippet: The mean (SD) differences in annualized growth velocity across each integer age (6-16 years) between treated and untreated children were 1.84 (0.38) cm/year in boys and 1.44 (0.63) cm/year in girls.

evidence.reference_title: Sustained growth-promoting effects of vosoritide in children with achondroplasia from an ongoing phase 3 extension study.
evidence.snippet: Vosoritide treatment was well tolerated and had sustained growth-promoting effects in children with achondroplasia treated for up to 6 years.

nodes.description: The most common causative mutation, accounting for over 95% of cases.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the transmembrane domain of FGFR3 cause the most common genetic form of dwarfism, achondroplasia.
evidence.snippet: Both mutations result in the substitution of an arginine residue for a glycine at position 380 of the mature protein, which is in the transmembrane domain of FGFR3.
